page: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance type built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The hardware configuration delivers 64 vCPUs, derived from 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, alongside 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz. This configuration provides a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core CPU and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests, PassMark CPU benchmarks, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only cached small block speeds reaching 4711.73 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 212.09 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong performance in database operations, software compilation, and Redis SET operations, which reached 70.49 million operations per second. However, single-core benchmarks and small-model LLM inference speeds fall into the average tier, while medium-model LLM prompt processing is weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i-flex.16xlarge offers a high-density resource profile for general-purpose workloads without the premium of specialized hardware. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instruction sets provides hardware-level acceleration for cryptography and vector math. This instance is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and Redis caching layers.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.16xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by high scores in PassMark CPU, software compilation, and Redis SET operations. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. It includes advanced CPU flags such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX for accelerated vector and matrix operations. While single-core benchmarks and LLM inference speeds are average to weak, its high memory bandwidth and multi-threaded capabilities make it highly efficient for database operations, web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong multi-core performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs and 256 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring sixty-four Intel Xeon vCPUs and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 8488C processor with AVX-512 and AMX extensions
Strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for databases, Redis caching, and software compilation
